Finance & Economy.headline: Bank of Portugal cuts 2026 GDP growth forecast
Finance & Economy.detail: The Bank of Portugal has decreased its economic growth forecast for 2026 from 2.3% to 1.8%, citing the economic impact of military strikes against Iran and severe weather conditions that affected Portugal. The inflation forecast has been revised upwards to 2.8% for 2026.

Finance & Economy.headline: Russia's Putin limits cash and gold exports
Finance & Economy.detail: President Vladimir Putin has signed decrees restricting cash and gold exports as part of efforts to combat the shadow economy and curb capital flight; rubles exceeding $100,000 and gold bars weighing more than 100 grams are restricted. The decree restricting ruble cash exports goes into effect on April 1st, while the decree restricting gold exports starts May 1st.

Finance & Economy.headline: OECD to release Interim Economic Outlook on Thursday 26 March 2026
Finance & Economy.detail: The OECD is set to release its Interim Economic Outlook today, March 26, 2026, covering various topics, including finance, investment, governance, health, and trade. The report will also address featured topics like artificial intelligence and climate change.

Finance & Economy.headline: Kuaishou CEO Confident in More than Doubling KLING AI Revenue This Yr
Finance & Economy.detail: Kuaishou's CEO expresses strong confidence in more than doubling the year-over-year revenue growth for KLING AI, based on current growth momentum and commercialization progress. The company's cumulative performance is down 17.04% YTD.

Finance & Economy.headline: ASX 200 set to rise following good night on Wall Street
Finance & Economy.detail: The Australian share market is expected to open higher following a positive session on Wall Street, with SPI futures indicating a 0.25% increase. Oil prices fell overnight due to optimism about a potential US-Iran peace deal, which could impact ASX 200 energy shares.

Technology.headline: Intel, AMD Reportedly Set to Raise CPU Prices By Up to 15%
Technology.detail: Both Intel and AMD are expected to increase CPU prices, potentially by as much as 15%, impacting consumers and manufacturers alike.

Technology.headline: Amazon's Big Spring Sale Has Huge Laptop and Desktop Deals From Acer, Apple, Asus, and More
Technology.detail: The Amazon Big Spring Sale includes significant discounts on laptops and desktops from major brands like Acer, Apple, and Asus.

Technology.headline: An Eye-Popping Deal: These Carrera Smart Glasses Are Under $150 During Amazon's Big Spring Sale
Technology.detail: Carrera Smart Glasses are available for under $150 as part of Amazon's Big Spring Sale.

Technology.headline: Beyond the chatbot: At GeekWire summit, AI leaders say the era of autonomous agents is already here.
Technology.detail: AI leaders at the GeekWire summit discussed the rapid development and implementation of autonomous agents across various industries.

Technology.headline: Google Primes Chrome for a Post-Quantum World
Technology.detail: Google is testing a new certificate architecture in Chrome designed to protect HTTPS and TLS connections from future quantum computing threats.

Science.headline: Scientists call out health-harming corporations driving rise in chronic disease
Science.detail: A group of scientists is criticizing corporations whose products and practices contribute to the increase in chronic diseases worldwide. They are urging for stricter regulations and greater corporate accountability.

Science.headline: Seismic activity in California varies with the seasons
Science.detail: Seasonal changes influence seismic activity in California, according to new research. The study reveals a correlation between the wet and dry seasons and the frequency of earthquakes.

Science.headline: Canadian orangutan scientist Biruté Galdikas dead at 79
Science.detail: Biruté Mary Galdikas, a Canadian scientist renowned for her dedication to studying and conserving orangutans, has passed away. She was the last surviving member of the esteemed primatologist trio known as the "trimates," which included Jane Goodall and Diane Fossey.

Science.headline: Scientists are racing to save Australia's 'zombie tree' from a fast-spreading fungal disease
Science.detail: Researchers are working to protect a unique Australian tree species, nicknamed the 'zombie tree', from a rapidly spreading fungal disease that threatens its existence. The effort involves studying the disease and implementing conservation strategies.

Science.headline: Flower nectar often contains small amounts of alcohol
Science.detail: New findings suggest that flower nectar naturally contains small amounts of alcohol, meaning pollinators like hummingbirds are consuming it throughout the day. Despite the alcohol consumption, these pollinators show no signs of intoxication, which suggests an evolutionary adaptation.

Health.headline: Resident doctors in England to begin six-day strike after rejecting offer in pay dispute
Health.detail: Resident doctors in England are commencing a six-day strike due to the rejection of a pay offer, escalating the ongoing dispute over compensation and working conditions.

Health.headline: Kent meningitis outbreak prompts rush for routine vaccinations in England
Health.detail: Following a meningitis outbreak in Kent, there has been a surge in demand for routine vaccinations as health officials work to contain the spread and protect the population.

Health.headline: US left without functioning vaccine panel as adviser says 'drama distracts'
Health.detail: The US is currently without a fully operational vaccine panel, due to an advisor stepping down citing that ongoing 'drama' is creating distractions and hindering progress.

Health.headline: Not just in your head: Here's how stress causes eczema flare-ups
Health.detail: A new study explores the biological mechanisms by which stress can trigger and exacerbate eczema flare-ups, highlighting the connection between mental health and skin conditions.

Health.headline: 29 infections and diseases may raise dementia risk, new study says
Health.detail: Research indicates that nearly 30 different infections and diseases could potentially elevate the risk of developing dementia, suggesting a broader range of factors may contribute to the condition than previously understood.

Sports.headline: First Khelo India Tribal Games begin today in Chhattisgarh
Sports.detail: The inaugural Khelo India Tribal Games are commencing today in Chhattisgarh, promoting sports among tribal communities. The event aims to encourage participation and showcase talent from tribal regions in various sports disciplines.

Sports.headline: WNBA sets April 3 expansion draft for new teams in Portland and Toronto
Sports.detail: The WNBA is preparing for its expansion draft on April 3, welcoming the new teams from Portland and Toronto into the league. This move marks a significant step in the growth and reach of women's basketball in North America.

Sports.headline: Padres pitcher Yu Darvish is starting season on restricted list while recovering from elbow surgery
Sports.detail: Yu Darvish, a pitcher for the San Diego Padres, will begin the season on the restricted list as he continues his recovery from elbow surgery. The team is taking precautions to ensure his full recovery before he returns to the field.

Sports.headline: Everton exploring legal options over lack of sporting sanctions against Chelsea
Sports.detail: Everton is considering legal action regarding the perceived lack of sporting sanctions against Chelsea. This follows Chelsea's fine for Abramovich-era payments, which Everton seemingly feels is insufficient compared to their own sanctions.

Sports.headline: Tolentino tempers expectations for Pinoy cyclists at Asian track championships
Sports.detail: Bambol Tolentino is managing expectations for Filipino cyclists participating in the Asian Track Championships. He is setting realistic goals for the team as they compete in the event.

Entertainment.headline: Tom Holland Declares Zendaya 'Looks Amazing' as They Step Out in Paris Amid Marriage Speculation
Entertainment.detail: Tom Holland tells fans Zendaya 'looks amazing' as they step out ahead of her "The Drama' premiere in Paris amid marriage speculation.

Entertainment.headline: JAY-Z Breaks Silence on Being Accused in Diddy Lawsuits
Entertainment.detail: JAY-Z breaks his silence about being named in a 2024 sexual misconduct lawsuit tied to Sean 'Diddy' Combes in a new 'GQ' interview.

Entertainment.headline: Bill Cosby Found Liable in Sexual Assault Case, Owes Victim $59M
Entertainment.detail: Bill Cosby is ordered to pay $59 million in damages to Donna Motsinger for drugging and assaulting her in 1972.

Entertainment.headline: Backlash mounts over twist in Robert Pattinson Zendaya romcom The Drama.
Entertainment.detail: Details regarding a twist in the Robert Pattinson and Zendaya romcom, The Drama, have caused backlash.

Entertainment.headline: Duffy to tell story of her kidnapping and rape ordeal in new Disney+ documentary.
Entertainment.detail: The Welsh musician Duffy is set to release a Disney+ documentary where she will share her experience of being kidnapped and raped.

Education.headline: Graduate Education Faces Notable Grade Inflation
Education.detail: Analysis of two decades of student data at a large U.S. university suggests that grade inflation exists in graduate education, with no overall difference seen for STEM versus non-STEM programs.

Education.headline: Ohio State University selects new president after Ted Carter's resignation
Education.detail: Ohio State University has selected a new president following Ted Carter's unexpected resignation, signaling a significant transition in leadership amidst financial challenges.

Education.headline: Griffith leaps in 2026 QS subject rankings
Education.detail: Griffith University is excelling across a broad range of subjects including Social Sciences and Management, Sport, Arts and Humanities, and Life Sciences and Medicine in the QS World University Rankings by Subject 2026.

Education.headline: Class Arrangements for Thursday and Friday, 26-27 March 2026 (HE Advisory)
Education.detail: Undergraduate and graduate classes of the Schools of Education and Learning Design at Ateneo de Manila University will have special arrangements due to a nationwide transportation strike on March 26-27.

Education.headline: States say Education Department not following mental health grant orders
Education.detail: The U.S. Department of Education is providing mental health grant recipients with only six months of funding instead of a full year, according to plaintiff states in recent court documents.

Lifestyle.headline: JetBlue flight turns back after striking a coyote on the runway: 'We thought it was a joke'
Lifestyle.detail: A JetBlue flight was forced to turn around after hitting a coyote on the runway, initially leading passengers to believe it was a joke.

Lifestyle.headline: Major airline tightens window for passengers to seek compensation after baggage delays
Lifestyle.detail: A major airline has reduced the time frame in which passengers can file for compensation due to delayed baggage.

Lifestyle.headline: Greater weight loss promised by higher-dose Wegovy shot, now approved by FDA
Lifestyle.detail: The FDA has approved Wegovy HD, a higher dose of semaglutide, for weight loss in adults with obesity, offering the potential for greater weight reduction, though experts caution about side effects.

Lifestyle.headline: Woman works through Dhurandhar 2 screening, Internet Says, "Gone Too Far"
Lifestyle.detail: A woman went viral for working through a Dhurandhar 2 screening, prompting reactions online.

Lifestyle.headline: Inside Vijay-Rashmika's Thailand Buddymoon At Rs 2.3 Lakh-A-Night Villa
Lifestyle.detail: Reportedly, Vijay and Rashmika enjoyed a buddymoon in Thailand at a villa costing Rs 2.3 Lakh a night.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Clamping down on spies in military
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Control Yuan urged the judiciary to impose stricter punishments on military personnel convicted of spying for China and called on the armed forces to strengthen the management of classified information after investigating two cases involving military personnel. The editorial likely discusses the implications and necessity of these measures for Taiwan's national security.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: When a political party loses direction
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This opinion piece discusses the increasing pressure on political parties in Taiwan to clarify their strategic positions amidst growing uncertainty in US-China relations. The author likely analyzes the challenges and potential consequences of this situation for Taiwan's political landscape.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Japanese PM will not cross the line
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This article discusses whether Japan would send naval forces to the Strait of Hormuz when Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi stood beside US President Donald Trump at the White House. The author likely analyzes the implications for Japan and Taiwan, considering regional security.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Supporting energy diversification
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This piece likely discusses Taiwan's historical dependence on imported oil and the government's plans to mitigate risk in its energy strategy, including the Jinshan Nuclear Power Plant. The author likely advocates for further diversification and analyzes the challenges and opportunities in Taiwan's energy sector.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Is honesty the best policy for the international order?
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This article poses a question about honesty in the international order. The author likely examines the role of transparency, diplomacy, and ethics in global politics, and analyzes the consequences of honesty (or lack thereof) in international relations.

World.headline: China Launches Two New Satellites
World.detail: China launched a Long March 2D carrier rocket on Thursday, sending two new satellites, Siwei Gaojing-2 05 and Siwei Gaojing-2 06, into their preset orbit from the Taiyuan Satellite Launch Center.

World.headline: Trump Says Iran Negotiators Fear Being 'Killed by Own People'
World.detail: US President Donald Trump insisted Wednesday that Iran was taking part in peace talks but suggested Tehran's denials were because Iranian negotiators fear being killed by their own side.

World.headline: Asia Braces for Worst-Case Energy Scenarios as Iran War Drags On
World.detail: Governments across Asia are preparing for worst-case energy scenarios that could include a prolonged and severe disruption to supplies, even as the US draws up a plan to end the war in Iran.

World.headline: UK Forces Can Board Russian Shadow Fleet in British Waters: Downing Street
World.detail: The UK Armed Forces and law enforcement officers will now be able to interdict vessels sanctioned by the UK that are transiting through UK waters, in an effort to put a further stranglehold on the 'shadow fleet'.

World.headline: U.S. Links Security Guarantees to Ukraine Giving up Donbas, Zelenskyy Says
World.detail: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy stated that the U.S. is making its offer of security guarantees for a peace deal conditional on Kyiv ceding all of the country's eastern region of Donbas to Russia.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe says 15 of its citizens killed after fraudulent recruitment into Russia-Ukraine war
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe has reported that 15 of its citizens have died after being fraudulently recruited to fight in the Russia-Ukraine war, with the government working to repatriate 66 other citizens.

Zimbabwe.headline: New magistrates sworn in
Zimbabwe.detail: New magistrates have been officially sworn in, marking a development in the country's judicial system.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwean to lead Seychelles commission of inquiry
Zimbabwe.detail: A Zimbabwean has been appointed to lead a commission of inquiry in Seychelles, highlighting the country's expertise in various fields.

Zimbabwe.headline: MP arrested over ZWG$1.2m CDF scandal
Zimbabwe.detail: A Member of Parliament has been arrested in connection with a ZWG$1.2 million scandal related to the Constituency Development Fund, raising concerns about corruption.

Zimbabwe.headline: Serial armed robbers nabbed
Zimbabwe.detail: Authorities have successfully apprehended a group of serial armed robbers, bringing a sense of relief to communities affected by their criminal activities.
